runtime_policy: Trigger only mechanisms relevant to the current task.

mechanisms.name: ChatGPT judgment, Codex execution, user decision separation
mechanisms.trigger: every task with judgment or execution
mechanisms.done_when: roles and authority are explicit
mechanisms.blocked_if: authority is ambiguous
mechanisms.validation: role fields present in task contract

mechanisms.name: Static rules and dynamic facts separation
mechanisms.trigger: rule or fact write
mechanisms.done_when: content is in one canonical layer
mechanisms.blocked_if: static and dynamic content are mixed
mechanisms.review_condition: stale facts appear in static rules

mechanisms.name: Source authority and conflict resolution
mechanisms.trigger: sources disagree or a fact is promoted
mechanisms.done_when: objective and decision authority are separated
mechanisms.blocked_if: source conflict remains unresolved

mechanisms.name: Cross-border task routing and state machine
mechanisms.trigger: every stateful task
mechanisms.done_when: entry and exit evidence satisfy the state
mechanisms.blocked_if: state or prerequisites are unclear

mechanisms.name: Six-layer requirement alignment
mechanisms.trigger: complex, modifying, or directional task
mechanisms.done_when: all six layers are explicit
mechanisms.blocked_if: a critical layer is missing

mechanisms.name: Ambiguous-goal clarification gate
mechanisms.trigger: ambiguous goal, benchmark, quality, or direction language
mechanisms.done_when: target, boundaries, and proof are clear
mechanisms.blocked_if: ambiguity changes the result materially

mechanisms.name: Implementation design before execution
mechanisms.trigger: file, tool, automation, or external-system change
mechanisms.done_when: primary route, fallback, validation, and rollback exist
mechanisms.blocked_if: implementation design is missing

mechanisms.name: Capability confirmation and probe separation
mechanisms.trigger: task depends on uncertain capability
mechanisms.human_gate: probe needing accounts, cost, or external write
mechanisms.done_when: capability state has evidence
mechanisms.blocked_if: required capability is unverified

mechanisms.name: Primary route, alternative route, and loss disclosure
mechanisms.trigger: fallback or route choice exists
mechanisms.done_when: loss is explicit and authorized
mechanisms.blocked_if: fallback is presented as original success

mechanisms.name: Cross-border task startup and impact check
mechanisms.trigger: any modifying, compliance, customer-facing, or external task
mechanisms.done_when: impact and permissions are explicit
mechanisms.blocked_if: scope or authority is unclear

mechanisms.name: Completion Relay
mechanisms.trigger: multi-deliverable task closeout
mechanisms.done_when: all required outputs and remaining work are reported
mechanisms.blocked_if: unreported required work remains

mechanisms.name: Completion truth
mechanisms.trigger: any completion claim
mechanisms.done_when: claim matches evidence layer
mechanisms.blocked_if: higher status is unsupported

mechanisms.name: Cross-border four-state completion
mechanisms.trigger: task closeout
mechanisms.done_when: one justified state is selected
mechanisms.blocked_if: state cannot be supported

mechanisms.name: Git decision and validation fields
mechanisms.trigger: formal Git commit
mechanisms.done_when: relevant decision fields are recorded
mechanisms.blocked_if: commit hides scope or untested gaps

mechanisms.name: GPT Project synchronization truth
mechanisms.trigger: GPT package generation or upload claim
mechanisms.done_when: status matches observed evidence
mechanisms.blocked_if: local package is claimed as UI sync

mechanisms.name: Original-source readback for retrieval results
mechanisms.trigger: search, summary, index, or AI retrieval informs action
mechanisms.human_gate: material decision based on uncertain source
mechanisms.done_when: original evidence is read and applicability recorded
mechanisms.blocked_if: only a summary or snippet is available

mechanisms.name: Dry-run and secret boundary
mechanisms.trigger: write, external action, paid call, or sensitive path
mechanisms.done_when: safe path is verified without secret exposure
mechanisms.blocked_if: secret access or unauthorized write is required

mechanisms.name: Cross-border human and AI permission boundary
mechanisms.trigger: market, compliance, price, contract, payment, sample, account, outreach, deletion, or scale decision
outputs: allowed AI preparation or human gate
mechanisms.done_when: authority and action scope are recorded
mechanisms.blocked_if: required approval is absent

mechanisms.name: L0-L3 engineering depth
mechanisms.trigger: task value, risk, repeatability, state, node, tool, evaluation, or failure-route complexity requires depth routing
mechanisms.human_gate: user authority remains required for business, external, or irreversible decisions
mechanisms.done_when: the lightest sufficient depth and validation path are explicit
mechanisms.blocked_if: complex work lacks required engineering contract or simple work is forced into L3
mechanisms.review_condition: task signals or maintenance evidence change
mechanisms.v2_redecision: P0 user authorization activates adapted depth routing without making L3 the default

mechanisms.name: Execution budget
mechanisms.trigger: mechanism, script, API, or automation proposal
mechanisms.done_when: mechanism cost is proportionate
mechanisms.blocked_if: system is heavier than the task

mechanisms.name: Collaboration effectiveness review
mechanisms.trigger: after the third real task and later reviews
outputs: simplify, merge, retain, or automate decision
mechanisms.done_when: effectiveness questions are answered
mechanisms.blocked_if: file count substitutes for outcome evidence
mechanisms.review_condition: third real task or recurring friction

mechanisms.name: Research Source-to-Action
mechanisms.trigger: external research may influence business action
mechanisms.done_when: original readback and action boundary are complete
mechanisms.blocked_if: only a summary, snippet, or inference exists

mechanisms.name: Single-primary-variable business experiment
mechanisms.trigger: channel, offer, content, outreach, or B2B/B2C comparison test
mechanisms.done_when: result and attribution limits are recorded
mechanisms.blocked_if: multiple uncontrolled variables prevent attribution

mechanisms.name: Video, TTS, editing, card, and review workflows
mechanisms.trigger: never
mechanisms.done_when: domain-specific mechanism remains excluded
mechanisms.blocked_if: old media workflow enters formal rules
mechanisms.review_condition: only if project scope materially changes

mechanisms.name: Fixed retrieval or external-AI technology dependency
mechanisms.trigger: never
mechanisms.done_when: no fixed vendor is mandatory
mechanisms.blocked_if: a legacy product becomes a default dependency
mechanisms.review_condition: validated project need and explicit approval

mechanisms.name: Legacy video-project statuses, paths, and conclusions
mechanisms.trigger: never
mechanisms.done_when: legacy facts remain outside current truth
mechanisms.blocked_if: legacy status or path enters current files

mechanisms.name: Conditional Git commit, push, and remote readback
mechanisms.trigger: authorized formal rule, fact, state, or decision change
mechanisms.done_when: local HEAD, origin/main, and ls-remote match
mechanisms.blocked_if: scope, push, or readback fails

mechanisms.name: Latest and history separation
mechanisms.trigger: state, decision, task, or rule update
mechanisms.done_when: current is canonical and history remains traceable
mechanisms.blocked_if: history overwrites current or vice versa
